title: Taking Turns
lesson: Waiting and taking turns means everyone gets a chance to play.
prompts: In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands turned toward two friends, one open hand raised in a clear open-palm wave at shoulder height, with a happy, curious expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stand close beside the swing facing the person from the input image, each raising one open hand to wave back, smiling warmly. Make the interaction read physically: each person's gaze, wave, and shoulders point toward the others. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the grass to the left of the swing seat, raising both hands in surprise, with a slightly surprised expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} crowd close on the grass to the right of the swing seat, each stretching one open hand toward the single swing seat, eager to get on. All three children stand with both feet planted on the grass, the one empty swing seat hanging between them. Make the interaction read physically: each child's gaze and reaching hand point toward the same single swing seat. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands in a small inward-facing circle, one open hand raised gently as if suggesting everyone wait their turn, with a calm, friendly smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stand back from the swing in that inward-facing circle, making small open-hand conversational gestures while looking at the person from the input image with patient, attentive expressions. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the others, with gaze and hands directed to the shared conversation. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands to one side clapping both hands together, with a cheerful, encouraging expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits on the swing seat gripping the two chains, legs stretched forward as she swings with a delighted grin, while {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands close behind the swing with both hands pressed gently against her back to push her, with an excited smile. Make the interaction read physically: the boy's hands touch the girl's back and his gaze follows the swing, while the person from the input image looks toward the swinging girl. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the swing seat, both hands gripping the two chains and legs stretched forward as they swing up, beaming with a joyful smile. Only this one child is in the frame, alone, with no second child, twin, or sibling. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — three children stand together in a single left-to-right row holding hands, swinging their joined hands gently, all laughing with big happy smiles. From left to right: first the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image, then {GENDER_F_AGE_06}, then {GENDER_M_AGE_06}, each holding the next child's hand in a connected row, all three facing forward together. Exactly three children in total, each a single distinct child — no fourth child, no duplicate or extra child, no twin. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}
negative_prompts: two children sitting on one swing, child sitting on the swing seat, child tangled in the swing chains, child hanging from the chains, extra swing, second swing, multiple swings
gists: At a sunny playground by a single swing, the child waves hello while two friends, a girl and a boy, wave back near the swing — the child arrives to play.
By the swing, the child raises both hands in surprise as the two friends crowd in, all reaching for the one swing seat at once — it's too crowded, nobody can swing.
By the swing, the child raises one hand calmly suggesting they wait their turn while the two friends stand back and discuss the idea with patient, attentive expressions.
By the swing, the child claps and cheers as the girl swings with a delighted grin and the boy gently pushes her from behind — a friend takes the first turn.
By the swing, the child sits on the swing seat gripping the chains mid-swing, beaming — it's the child's turn and they enjoy it.
By the swing, the child and the two friends hold hands in a row, all happy — everyone got a turn and had fun.
texts: {NAME} came to the playground and saw a swing swaying in the breeze. Two friends were there too. "Let's play!" said {NAME}, waving hello.
But everyone rushed to the swing at once — even {NAME}! "Hey, I can't get on!" said the boy. It was far too crowded, and nobody could swing.
"Let's take turns," said {NAME} gently, raising a hand. "Good idea," said the girl, and the friends stepped back and nodded.
One friend swung first — wheee! "Higher, higher!" cheered {NAME}, clapping, while the boy gave a gentle push. "This is so much fun!" laughed the girl.
Then it was {NAME}'s turn. "Here I go!" {NAME} held on tight and swung up high with a great big smile.
Everyone got a turn, and everyone had fun. "That was the best!" said {NAME}. They all held hands, as happy as can be. Taking turns means everyone gets to play.
